history.content:
CURRENT OBSERVATION:
??#...L
??#LLLL
??#...L
??#...L
??#...L
??#LLLL
??#^...

<thought>
I see a wall directly in front of me blocking the forward path, and to the right, there are multiple lava cells. I need to turn away from the wall to find a safe direction to move. Turning left seems safer as it may open up a path without lava immediately blocking.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
Now that I have turned left, I see that I am facing a wall and to my immediate right is a lava cell. Moving forward would be impossible because of the wall, and moving towards lava is deadly. I should turn left again to try facing the opposite direction where there might be a safer path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
After turning left twice, I am now facing away from the wall and lava. The cell in front of me appears to be a floor ('.') which is safe to move into. I will move forward to explore further and find a path to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation has not changed, meaning I likely did not move forward because there is a wall ('#') directly in front of me. I need to turn to find a clear path. Turning right will face me towards the open floor cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Now I see some more of the map. Lava (L) is to my right, and a wall (#) is directly in front of me, so moving forward is unsafe. I will turn left to face the safe floor cells ahead.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The observation has reset to an earlier view, indicating I might have turned or the environment rotated. The path in front is blocked by a wall. I will try turning left to look for an open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see lava cells (L) on my left and ahead to the left, as well as a wall (#) directly in front of me. The only direction that seems clear and safe is turning right. I will turn right to avoid lava and walls.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The observation indicates walls directly ahead and unknown cells around me. Since moving forward is blocked by a wall, I will try turning right to find a path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I see lava (L) to my right and floor cells in front of me with a wall (#) further ahead. The path to the right is dangerous due to lava, and moving forward seems clear for now. I will move forward to avoid lava and continue exploring.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation didn't change after moving forward, meaning I likely did not move because of a wall or obstacle directly ahead. Since the lava cell is still to my right and I cannot move forward, I will try turning left to explore another direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see walls to my right and some floor cells to my left and ahead, with no lava immediately visible. Turning left previously gave me this view, and I am at the bottom row. Moving forward will move me into a floor cell and seems safe compared to walls around. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
After moving forward, the observation remains the same, indicating I might be blocked by a wall or didn't move. Ahead is still a floor cell, but there is a wall at the right. I will try turning left to check another direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
There are lava cells to my left and ahead-left, making those directions risky. There is a wall just to my right and in front. The path directly forward is blocked by a wall. The only safe direction appears to be turning right to avoid lava and walls. I will turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
After turning right, the environment looks the same as before, with a wall to the right and a floor cell ahead. The forward cell is free, so I can move forward safely.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The forward cell is a floor, so I can continue moving forward safely. There is a wall just to my right, so turning right again would not be helpful. I'll move forward to make progress.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I see that the map hasn't changed and there's a wall to the right and floor ahead. Moving forward is safe and the most logical next step to proceed.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation remains the same, and moving forward is still the only safe option since there are walls on the sides. I will move forward once more.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The local observation has not changed and straight ahead is a free floor cell. Continuing forward seems like the best option to explore further.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I keep facing forward, but the view remains unchanged. There are walls blocking the left and right sides, and I am at the bottom center of the view. I might have reached a dead end or a corner. I should turn to explore other directions.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see lava (L) cells on the left side and a wall (#) on the right side. The path ahead appears to be clear floor cells. The goal is not visible yet. To avoid lava, I should continue moving forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The path directly ahead is blocked by walls. I need to turn to find a clear path since moving forward is not possible. Turning right may help me explore a new direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right and the cells directly in front and to the sides are walls. Since forward movement is blocked, I should turn right to explore new directions and possibly find a path around the walls.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The forward cell is a wall, so I cannot move forward. Turning left or right may help me find a path. Turning left might expose more open space as walls appear on the right side.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I'm facing up this time and the view ahead is blocked by walls. I cannot move forward. I will turn left to see if there is an opening on my left side.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The path directly ahead is blocked by a wall. To my left, there is lava, so moving left would be dangerous. The cells to my right appear open, so I will turn right to explore that direction for a safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>
